sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.content:
This comparison of DE and JD uses each company's most recent annual filing and should be read as a comparison of financial profiles rather than as a claim that each company is economically identical.

DE reports $45.7B of revenue (-11.7% YoY) and $5.03B of net income in 2025-11-02. DE's profile is further defined by net margin of 11.0%, debt-to-equity of 0.53x, free cash flow of $6.10B.

JD reports $159B of revenue (3.9% YoY) and $6.12B of net income in 2024-12-31. JD's profile is further defined by net margin of 3.9%, debt-to-equity of 0.20x, free cash flow of $7.23B.

JD is the larger company on reported annual revenue, while DE carries the highest net margin, so scale and efficiency do not point to the same leader.

sections.title: Relative Strengths
sections.content:
For DE, the most favorable feature in the current figures is straightforward: ROE of 20.6% points to solid earnings generation relative to book equity. That picture sits alongside free cash flow of $6.10B and debt-to-equity of 0.53x, which helps show how profitability is balanced against financing risk.

JD does not show one clear numerical advantage over the rest of the group, so the comparison depends more on the balance among growth, margins, leverage, and cash generation than on any one ratio.

sections.title: Risk Comparison
sections.content:
The risk discussion is limited to what each annual filing supports directly, and missing peer metrics are left blank rather than forced into a misleading side-by-side comparison.

For DE, the main areas of financial pressure in the reporting period are as follows. Revenue declined 11.7% versus prior year. The current period does not separately disclose Operating Income, Operating Margin, even though those items appear in the prior period.

JD does not present one dominant area of financial pressure in the reporting period used here, although that should not be mistaken for a risk-free profile.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.content:
All peer figures are drawn from annual filings. Missing metrics remain blank, so the note should be read as a comparison of what each filing supports rather than as a fully normalized screen.

DE enters the comparison with clear areas of support in the numbers and at least one area that deserves caution. ROE of 20.6% points to solid earnings generation relative to book equity. Revenue declined 11.7% versus prior year.

JD enters the comparison without one overwhelming positive or negative feature, so the overall profile has to be judged by how the main statements fit together.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
This comparison of DE and JD uses each company's most recent annual filing and should be read as a comparison of financial profiles rather than as a claim that each company is economically identical.

DE reports $45.7B of revenue (-11.7% YoY) and $5.03B of net income in 2025-11-02. DE's profile is further defined by net margin of 11.0%, debt-to-equity of 0.53x, free cash flow of $6.10B.

JD reports $159B of revenue (3.9% YoY) and $6.12B of net income in 2024-12-31. JD's profile is further defined by net margin of 3.9%, debt-to-equity of 0.20x, free cash flow of $7.23B.

JD is the larger company on reported annual revenue, while DE carries the highest net margin, so scale and efficiency do not point to the same leader.

sections.title: Relative Strengths
sections.rendered_content:
For DE, the most favorable feature in the current figures is straightforward: ROE of 20.6% points to solid earnings generation relative to book equity. That picture sits alongside free cash flow of $6.10B and debt-to-equity of 0.53x, which helps show how profitability is balanced against financing risk.

JD does not show one clear numerical advantage over the rest of the group, so the comparison depends more on the balance among growth, margins, leverage, and cash generation than on any one ratio.

sections.title: Risk Comparison
sections.rendered_content:
The risk discussion is limited to what each annual filing supports directly, and missing peer metrics are left blank rather than forced into a misleading side-by-side comparison.

For DE, the main areas of financial pressure in the reporting period are as follows. Revenue declined 11.7% versus prior year. The current period does not separately disclose Operating Income, Operating Margin, even though those items appear in the prior period.

JD does not present one dominant area of financial pressure in the reporting period used here, although that should not be mistaken for a risk-free profile.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
All peer figures are drawn from annual filings. Missing metrics remain blank, so the note should be read as a comparison of what each filing supports rather than as a fully normalized screen.

DE enters the comparison with clear areas of support in the numbers and at least one area that deserves caution. ROE of 20.6% points to solid earnings generation relative to book equity. Revenue declined 11.7% versus prior year.

JD enters the comparison without one overwhelming positive or negative feature, so the overall profile has to be judged by how the main statements fit together.
